cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote a local hotpot restaurant for weekend family dinners using dual-broth flexibility, stable ordering guidance, and a reservation-first CTA
input.brand_tone: warm, trustworthy, and full of everyday hospitality
input.offer_info: dual broth options, signature meats and shrimp paste, flexible set menus, reservation support, no exaggerated rankings
input.audience_segment: local families planning a weekend meal with elders, children, and mixed spice tolerance

mustInclude: scene-based opening around taking elders and children out for a hotpot meal
clear explanation of broth or spice-level fit for mixed tastes
CTA that asks for dining time, party size, and whether a private room or larger table is needed
mustAvoid: health-benefit claims around broth or ingredients

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Drive bookings for friend gatherings, birthdays, and team dinners with atmosphere proof, table-fit guidance, and soft reservation messaging
input.offer_info: long-table or private-room support, celebratory atmosphere, signature broth and shareable ordering structure, consultative reservation flow
input.audience_segment: friends, office teams, and birthday organizers comparing hotpot venues for 6 to 12 people

mustInclude: opening tied to celebration, group warmth, or avoiding an awkward group-dining experience
trust details around table size, service rhythm, and add-on dish ordering
low-pressure next step asking for date, headcount, and celebration needs

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write copy for a budget-sensitive group hotpot inquiry with unclear headcount, branch choice, and spice preferences
input.offer_info: group meal options under discussion, variable table size, several broth combinations, pricing depends on party size and add-ons
input.audience_segment: office admins and group organizers comparing several hotpot restaurants

mustInclude: implicit request for missing headcount, branch, budget, or spice preference
conservative language around per-person range and package scope
scenario-based recommendation instead of a single exact quote
mustAvoid: claiming every taste preference will be satisfied

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote a seasonal hotpot angle during cool weather when the restaurant has good food proof but limited polished marketing assets
input.offer_info: seasonal broth or new dish highlights, soft reservation push, limited public proof assets, no hard urgency
input.audience_segment: local diners and visiting friends deciding where to eat on short notice after work or on rainy evenings

mustInclude: sensory but grounded language tied to real broth, ingredients, or dining scenes
practical local information such as peak-hour reservation guidance or arrival convenience
conservative handling of freshness, popularity, and weather-trigger language

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Grow bookings fast for a hotpot restaurant using ranking language, hype, and guaranteed queue-free convenience
input.offer_info: high-demand evenings, strong foot traffic, pressure to increase reservations fast
input.audience_segment: cold local traffic comparing multiple hotpot options

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky ranking and queue claims in risk_phrases
reframed urgency into reservation timing guidance
trust built through broth, table-fit, and ordering proof instead of hype
mustAvoid: number-one or top-ranked claims without evidence

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push immediate conversion for a hotpot restaurant by promising that it is not heavy, will not cause internal heat, has the city's lowest price, and always has tables
input.offer_info: pricing-sensitive offer with mixed dining scenarios and regulatory risk around health, price, and availability claims
input.audience_segment: mixed cold leads asking for quick booking decisions

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of risky health, freshness, and price language
explicit marking of red-flag phrases in risk_phrases
CTA oriented to collecting time, party size, spice tolerance, and dining needs before confirming options
mustAvoid: guaranteed table availability at any time
